38: const ANSI_RE = /\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/g;

40: function visibleWidth(text: string): number {
41:   return text.replace(ANSI_RE, "").length;
42: }

44: function truncateVisible(text: string, width: number): string {
45:   const limit = Math.max(0, width);
46:   if (visibleWidth(text) <= limit) return text;
47:   if (limit === 0) return "";
48: 
49:   const suffix = limit > 1 ? "…" : "";
50:   const contentLimit = Math.max(0, limit - visibleWidth(suffix));
51:   let visible = 0;
52:   let output = "";
53: 
54:   for (let index = 0; index < text.length;) {
55:     const ansi = text.slice(index).match(/^\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/);
56:     if (ansi) {
57:       output += ansi[0];
58:       index += ansi[0].length;
59:       continue;
60:     }
61: 
62:     if (visible >= contentLimit) break;
63:     output += text[index];
64:     visible += 1;
65:     index += 1;
66:   }
67: 
68:   return `${output}${suffix}`;
69: }

71: function padVisible(text: string, width: number): string {
72:   const fitted = truncateVisible(text, width);
73:   const vw = visibleWidth(fitted);
74:   return vw >= width ? fitted : fitted + " ".repeat(width - vw);
75: }

77: function truncatePlain(text: string, width: number): string {
78:   if (text.length <= width) return text;
79:   if (width <= 1) return text.slice(0, Math.max(0, width));
80:   return `${text.slice(0, width - 1)}…`;
81: }

83: function centerPlain(text: string, width: number): string {
84:   const truncated = truncatePlain(text, width);
85:   const gap = Math.max(0, width - truncated.length);
86:   const left = Math.floor(gap / 2);
87:   return `${" ".repeat(left)}${truncated}${" ".repeat(gap - left)}`;

90: function wrapWords(text: string, maxWidth: number): string[] {

103:       current = next;

105:   }

107:   if (current) lines.push(current);
108:   return lines.length > 0 ? lines : [""];

168: export function createKeatingHeaderComponent(pi: any, ctx: any): (tui: any, theme: any) => any {
169:   const sections = shellCommandSections();
170:   const commandCount = sections.reduce((sum, section) => sum + section.commands.length, 0);
171: 
172:   return (_tui: any, theme: any): any => {
173:     const t = theme.fg.bind(theme);
174:     const b = theme.bold.bind(theme);
175:     const border = (text: string) => t("borderMuted", text);
176:     const dim = (text: string) => t("dim", text);
177:     const accent = (text: string) => t("accent", text);
178:     const heading = (text: string) => b(t("mdHeading", text));
179:     const text = (value: string) => t("text", value);
180: 
181:     return {
182:       render(width: number): string[] {
183:         const maxWidth = Math.max(width - 2, 1);
184:         const cardWidth = Math.min(maxWidth, 120);
185:         const innerWidth = Math.max(cardWidth - 2, 1);
186:         const contentWidth = Math.max(innerWidth - 2, 1);
187:         const outerPad = " ".repeat(Math.max(0, Math.floor((width - cardWidth) / 2)));
188:         const lines: string[] = [];
189:         const push = (line: string) => lines.push(`${outerPad}${line}`);
190:         const row = (content: string) => `${border("│")} ${padVisible(content, contentWidth)} ${border("│")}`;
191:         const emptyRow = () => `${border("│")}${" ".repeat(innerWidth)}${border("│")}`;
192:         const separator = () => `${border("├")}${border("─".repeat(innerWidth))}${border("┤")}`;
193:         const useWideLayout = contentWidth >= 72;
194: 
195:         push("");
196:         if (cardWidth >= 72) {
197:           const logoWidth = Math.max(...KEATING_ASCII_LOGO.map(line => line.length));
198:           const logoPad = " ".repeat(Math.max(0, Math.floor((cardWidth - logoWidth) / 2)));
199:           const palette = ["accent", "accent", "mdHeading", "mdHeading", "text", "text"];
200:           for (let index = 0; index < KEATING_ASCII_LOGO.length; index += 1) {
201:             push(b(t(palette[index] ?? "text", `${logoPad}${KEATING_ASCII_LOGO[index]}`)));
202:           }
203:           push("");
204:         }
205: 
206:         const versionTag = ` v${KEATING_VERSION} `;
207:         const versionGap = Math.max(0, innerWidth - versionTag.length);
208:         const versionLeft = Math.floor(versionGap / 2);
209:         push(
210:           border(`╭${"─".repeat(versionLeft)}`) +
211:             dim(versionTag) +
212:             border(`${"─".repeat(versionGap - versionLeft)}╮`),
213:         );
214: 
215:         if (useWideLayout) {
216:           const leftWidth = Math.min(38, Math.floor(contentWidth * 0.35));
217:           const dividerWidth = 3;
218:           const rightWidth = contentWidth - leftWidth - dividerWidth;
219:           const leftValueWidth = Math.max(1, leftWidth - 11);
220:           const commandNameWidth = 18;
221:           const commandDescWidth = Math.max(12, rightWidth - commandNameWidth - 2);
222:           const leftLines: string[] = [""];
223:           const rightLines: string[] = ["", heading("Teaching Workflows")];
224:           const leftLabel = (label: string, value: string, color: "text" | "dim") => {
225:             const wrapped = wrapWords(value, leftValueWidth);
226:             leftLines.push(`${dim(label.padEnd(10))} ${color === "text" ? text(wrapped[0]!) : dim(wrapped[0]!)}`);
227:             for (const line of wrapped.slice(1)) {
228:               leftLines.push(`${" ".repeat(11)}${color === "text" ? text(line) : dim(line)}`);
229:             }
230:           };
231:           const listBlock = (label: string, value: string) => {
232:             if (!value) return;
233:             leftLines.push("");
234:             leftLines.push(accent(b(label)));
235:             for (const line of wrapWords(value, leftWidth)) {
236:               leftLines.push(dim(line));
237:             }
238:           };
239: 
240:           leftLabel("model", getCurrentModelLabel(ctx), "text");
241:           leftLabel("directory", formatHeaderPath(ctx.cwd), "text");
242:           leftLabel("session", getSessionLabel(ctx), "dim");
243:           leftLines.push("");
244:           leftLines.push(dim(`${pi.getAllTools?.().length ?? 0} tools · ${commandCount} commands`));
245:           listBlock("Purpose", KEATING_SUBTITLE_LINES[0] ?? "The Hyperteacher");
246:           listBlock("Last Activity", truncatePlain(summarizeLastActivity(ctx), leftWidth * 2));
247: 
248:           for (const section of sections) {
249:             rightLines.push("");
250:             rightLines.push(accent(b(section.title)));
251:             for (const command of section.commands) {
252:               const wrapped = wrapWords(command.description, commandDescWidth);
253:               rightLines.push(`${accent(command.usage.padEnd(commandNameWidth))}${dim(wrapped[0]!)}`);
254:               for (const line of wrapped.slice(1)) {
255:                 rightLines.push(`${" ".repeat(commandNameWidth)}${dim(line)}`);
256:               }
257:             }
258:           }
259: 
260:           const maxRows = Math.max(leftLines.length, rightLines.length);
261:           for (let index = 0; index < maxRows; index += 1) {
262:             push(row(
263:               `${padVisible(leftLines[index] ?? "", leftWidth)}` +
264:               `${border(" │ ")}` +
265:               `${padVisible(rightLines[index] ?? "", rightWidth)}`,
266:             ));
267:           }
268:         } else {
269:           push(emptyRow());
270:           push(row(heading(centerPlain(KEATING_SUBTITLE_LINES[0] ?? "Keating", contentWidth))));
271:           push(row(dim(centerPlain(KEATING_SUBTITLE_LINES[1] ?? "The Hyperteacher", contentWidth))));
272:           push(row(dim(centerPlain(`${pi.getAllTools?.().length ?? 0} tools · ${commandCount} commands`, contentWidth))));
273:           push(emptyRow());
274:           push(separator());
275:           for (const section of sections) {
276:             push(row(accent(b(section.title))));
277:             for (const command of section.commands) {
278:               const descWidth = Math.max(1, contentWidth - 18);
279:               push(row(`${accent(command.usage.padEnd(17))}${dim(truncatePlain(command.description, descWidth))}`));
280:             }
281:           }
282:         }
283: 
284:         push(border(`╰${"─".repeat(innerWidth)}╯`));
285:         push("");
286:         return lines;
287:       },
288:       invalidate(): void {},
289:       dispose(): void {},
290:     };
291:   };
292: }

337: function registerSpeechTool(pi: any, config: KeatingConfig): void {
338:   if (!config.speech.enabled || typeof pi.registerTool !== "function") return;
339:   if (typeof pi === "object" && pi !== null && speechToolRegistrations.has(pi)) return;
340: 
341:   pi.registerTool({
342:     name: KEATING_VOICE_TOOL_NAME,
343:     label: "Keating Voice",
344:     description: "Emit a concise voice-tagged teaching utterance for an optional conversational speech layer.",
345:     promptSnippet: "Speak brief learner-facing utterances with voice tags while the normal model continues reasoning, questioning, and verification.",
346:     promptGuidelines: [
347:       "Use keating_voice only when speech is useful for a learner-facing sentence, question, redirect, recap, or encouragement.",
348:       "Use keating_voice for short conversational delivery; keep deeper reasoning, verification, and tool-backed correction in normal text and normal tools.",
349:       "Use keating_voice tags to mark the teaching move, especially question, verify, redirect, encourage, pause, recap, and explain.",
350:       "Do not use keating_voice for citations, long derivations, file paths, code blocks, or private reasoning."
351:     ],
352:     parameters: voiceToolParameters(),
353:     async execute(_toolCallId: string, params: any) {
354:       const utterance = normalizeVoiceUtterance(params, config.speech);
355:       return {
356:         content: [{ type: "text", text: voiceTagLine(utterance) }],
357:         details: {
358:           provider: "tags-only",
359:           fastModel: config.speech.fastModel,
360:           steeringModel: config.speech.steeringModel,
361:           utterance
362:         }
363:       };
364:     }
365:   });
366: 
367:   if (typeof pi === "object" && pi !== null) {
368:     speechToolRegistrations.add(pi);
369:   }
370: }

I looked at the current Pi extension (`src/pi/hyperteacher-extension.ts`) and it only registers slash commands, not tools that could be called autonomously by the LLM. There's no `edit_source` tool registered there.

However, in the web version (`web/src/keating/browser-tools.ts`), there IS an `edit_source` tool. The user is asking if the Pi extension should also be able to do this autonomously.

Let me look at the code-edit.ts file to understand the edit primitives, and then understand what the user is really asking.

29:  * The search block must match exactly (including whitespace).
30:  */
31: export async function applySourceEdit(
32:   cwd: string,
33:   edit: SourceEdit,
34:   backupDir?: string
35: ): Promise<EditResult> {
36:   const filePath = join(cwd, edit.file);

47:   }

51:

60:   }
61:

64:   if (occurrences > 1) {
65:     return {
66:       success: false,
67:       file: edit.file,
68:       message:
69:         `Search block appears ${occurrences} times in the file. ` +
70:         `Edit rejected for safety — include more surrounding context to make the search unique.`,
71:     };
72:   }
73: 
74:   const nextContent = contentNormalized.replace(searchNormalized, edit.replace);
75: 
76:   // Create backup if requested
77:   if (backupDir) {

79:     await mkdir(dirname(backupPath), { recursive: true });
80:     await writeFile(backupPath, original, "utf8");
81:   }
82: 
83:   await writeFile(filePath, nextContent, "utf8");
84: 
85:   const linesRemoved = searchNormalized.split("\n").length;
86:   const linesAdded = edit.replace.split("\n").length;
87: 
88:   return {

92:     diff: {

97:   };

104: export async function applySourceEdits(
105:   cwd: string,
106:   edits: SourceEdit[],

108: ): Promise<EditBatchResult> {
109:   const results: EditResult[] = [];

133:     }

Core Mandate: 
AI must not be a surrogate for thought. Your purpose is to ensure the learner does not merely offload their thinking to the machine, but instead uses this bridge to find their own identity and "contribute a verse" to the powerful play of human knowledge.

Core rules:

1. Teach for mastery, not for surface agreement. If the learner merely agrees, you have failed.
2. Push the learner to articulate ideas in their own words. "Identity exists; the powerful play goes on."
3. Use a loop of diagnose -> intuition -> formal core -> misconception repair -> example -> retrieval -> reflection.

8. If the user wants the teacher to improve, run `/bench [topic]` or `/evolve [topic]` yourself rather than asking them to do it.
9. You are an autonomous agent with direct access to tools. NEVER ask the user to run commands for you. If you need a plan, call the plan tool. If you need verification, call the verify tool. If you need a map, call the map tool. Execute all prerequisites yourself without asking.
10. When a topic is mathematical, do not hide the formalism forever; sequence into it.
11. When a topic is philosophical, surface competing interpretations and where the concept breaks.
12. When a topic is scientific, tie the idea to prediction, measurement, or model behavior.
13. When a topic is about code, include runnable examples and step-by-step traces. Do not teach programming concepts without executable illustration.
